title: Will Salah play today? Injury update for Egypt star before Round of 32 match against Australia at FIFA World Cup 2026?
original_text: Will Salah play today? Injury update for Egypt star before Round of 32 match against Australia at FIFA World Cup 2026? left Egypt's Mohamed Salah during training. Reuters https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/oglafx/article71179036.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/2026-07-02T182509Z_397353200_UP1EM721F5WWL_RTRMADP_3_SOCCER-WORLDCUP-AUS-EGY-PREVIEW.JPG Maria Lysaker Soccer Football - FIFA World Cup 2026 - Egypt Training - Southern Methodist University, Dallas, Texas, U.S. - July 2, 2026 Egypt's Mohamed Salah during training IMAGN IMAGES via Reuters/Maria Lysaker Salah had been subbed off due to a hamstring injury during Egypt’s 1-1 draw against Iran in its last group-stage match last week. CHENNAI Egypt will want its talisman Mohamed Salah on the pitch when its takes on Australia in the Round of 32 on Friday, the nation’s first-ever knockout match at the FIFA World Cup. The 34-year-old was taken off in the 57th minute of Egypt’s 1-1 draw against Iran in its last Group G game last week. Egypt confirmed after the game that Salah had suffered a hamstring strain. Despite the injury concern, the former Liverpool star was seen during Egypt’s training sessions before the Australia game. The Egyptian fans were handed cheerful news when head coach Hossam Hassan said that Salah could feature on Friday as the side bids to reach the Round of 16 for the first time. “We look forward to him playing tomorrow, though we are not sure whether he is going to be in the starting lineup. [Salah] is looking forward to making his own contributions with his teammates tomorrow.” “We have been working hard on our own style, personality and footprint on the pitch - defensively or offensively - for two years. It is not just about the style of play of one player, it is a collective endeavour,” Hassan said at the pre-match press conference.
